prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Combobox: button or input with aria-haspopup=listbox plus listbox/options Reference DOM (Meter self assessment): Self-assessment field uses meter element near text inputs Reference DOM (Datalist autocomplete): Location field uses input list= with datalist suggestions Reference DOM (React phone dial code only): react-phone-number-input style: separate dial code combobox and national number Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: readonly-prefill, aria-labelledby-hidden, duplicate-name-attrs, details-accordion, custom-combobox. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: unstyled div soup with inline styles only. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Meter self assessment; Datalist autocomplete; React phone dial code only.
